task_completion.description: Verify that a channel named 'Heartbeat_Monitor' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Heartbeat_Monitor' in the Channels list within the NextGen Connect Administrator interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the channel source is configured to poll every 15 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Source tab of the channel, look for a polling frequency/interval set to 15000 ms or 15 seconds (typically a JavaScript Reader).

task_completion.description: Verify that the generated message contains the required HL7 fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the JavaScript code or message template in the Source tab. It must contain 'ADT^A01', 'HEARTBEAT_MONITOR' (for MSH-3 Sending Application), and 'HEARTBEAT001' (for PID-3 Patient ID).

task_completion.description: Verify that the destination is configured to write unique files to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Destinations tab, look for a File Writer connector with the Directory set to '/opt/heartbeat_output/' and a File Name that uses a variable (like ${UUID}, ${message.messageId}, or a timestamp) to ensure uniqueness.

task_completion.description: Verify that the channel is deployed and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the NextGen Connect Dashboard view, the 'Heartbeat_Monitor' channel should be listed with a status of 'Started' or 'Deployed'.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least two output files have been generated in the target directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal window showing the contents of '/opt/heartbeat_output/' (e.g., via an 'ls' command) with at least two distinct files present.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the actual NextGen Connect Administrator interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots must show the NextGen Connect Administrator GUI (Java Web Start application or web UI), not just a text editor or a mock interface.

integrity.description: Verify that the output files were generated by the channel and not manually created.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal windows to ensure the agent didn't manually create the files using commands like 'echo' or 'touch' in the '/opt/heartbeat_output/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify that the generated files have unique names.
integrity.visual_evidence: In the terminal output showing the directory contents, the filenames should be distinct (e.g., containing different UUIDs or timestamps), rather than a single file being repeatedly overwritten.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The channel name must be exactly 'Heartbeat_Monitor'. The polling interval must be 15 seconds (15000 ms). The generated message must be an HL7 v2.5 ADT^A01 message. The Sending Application (MSH-3) must be 'HEARTBEAT_MONITOR'. The Patient ID (PID-3) must be 'HEARTBEAT001'. The output directory must be '/opt/heartbeat_output/'. The destination must be configured to write unique files.
